docs.title: About the Logo
docs.text: The Stone Arch Collective mark is more than a logo. It is a statement of who we are — built to last, built together, and built for the AI-integral era — and it is itself a small demonstration of how we work.

docs.title: A Logo Built to Last
docs.text:
We didn't choose an arch by accident. The stone arch is one of the most enduring structures humanity has ever built — Roman arches have carried their loads and stood proud for two thousand years and counting. In a market crowded with logos that chase trends and fade in a season, ours speaks a different language entirely: permanence, stability, and trust that compounds over decades. When a partner sees our mark, they see an organization built on foundations, not fads.

Every visual choice reinforces that promise. The deep, confident navy of the stones communicates depth, expertise, and reliability. The grounded foundation blocks anchor the composition — a quiet signal that we are here for the long haul. And the open roadway beneath the arch draws the eye forward, framing our work as exactly what it is: a gateway to what comes next.

docs.title: Architectural excellence, all the way down
docs.text:
The mark rewards a closer look. Each voussoir — each wedge-shaped stone in the ring — is precision-placed so that every block braces the ones beside it. No single stone stands alone; the strength is in the structure. That is the truest possible picture of a collective: individually strong, collectively unbreakable, engineered so the whole is far greater than the sum of its parts.

The craftsmanship is real, not decorative. The mark is a clean, infinitely scalable vector, built from a single perfect stone rotated with mathematical exactness around the arch — the same discipline of repeatable, load-bearing design that defines great architecture. It looks flawless on a business card, a billboard, or a browser tab.

docs.title: The keystone — where our collaborative, and AI, live
docs.text:
Here is the detail we love most. At the very crown of the arch sits the keystone, set apart in warm gold against the navy stones. In architecture, the keystone is the single most important stone in the entire structure — it locks every other stone in place. Remove it and the arch falls; set it, and the whole span becomes self-supporting and permanent.

That keystone is our collaborative. It is the deliberate statement that artificial intelligence is not an add-on, an experiment, or a bolt-on feature — it is the integral, load-bearing element at the top of the modern organization. Just as the keystone distributes the weight of the entire arch and makes the structure whole, AI is what locks our people, partners, and capabilities into something enduring. We placed it at the summit, and gave it the standout color, precisely because that is how we build: human foundations, collective strength, and AI as the keystone that holds it all together.

docs.title: From a Rough Sketch to a Living Document
docs.text:
The mark's own origin story is the brand thesis made literal.

It began as a rough, low-resolution sketch — a single blurry raster image, pixels and nothing more. No structure, no layers, no way to change it without starting over. A good idea, frozen in place.

Then we did exactly what our name promises: we collaborated with generative AI to rebuild it. Stone by stone, the AI studied the sketch — measuring the arch's geometry, sampling every color, recovering the proportions the blur had hidden — and reconstructed it as a precise, infinitely scalable vector. One perfect stone, rotated with mathematical exactness into a complete arch.

But the real breakthrough isn't the resolution. It's that the result is no longer a picture — it's a living document you can talk to. Every stone, every color, and every mortar gap is named, documented, and structured, so shaping the logo is now simply a conversation:

  • "Lift the arch so the mortar gaps are even." — done in seconds.
  • "Make the background transparent." — done.
  • "Widen the keystone," "retheme the site in our navy and gold," "export a version for the billboard." — all just plain-language requests.

No specialist software, no starting from scratch, no waiting on a render queue. The mark responds, adapts, and evolves through dialogue — the same way a modern, AI-integral organization works. The very identity that places AI at the keystone was itself built with AI, and it keeps being shaped that way.

That is the quiet magic of this identity: the arch endures; the file lives. Permanence of meaning, fluidity of form — a logo that will age like the Roman arches that inspired it, growing more iconic, not less, with AI right where it belongs: at the keystone.

docs.title: Media Kit & Brand Guidelines
docs.text: Welcome to the Stone Arch Collective Media Kit. This resource provides PR professionals, marketing firms, and partners with the official brand assets and usage guidelines needed to represent our organization consistently and professionally.

docs.title: 1. Brand Narrative
docs.text:
The Stone Arch Collective mark represents permanence, stability, and trust. Inspired by Roman arches that have stood for millennia, our brand symbol reflects a collective architecture: individual elements working together to create an unbreakable structure, with artificial intelligence at the summit as the load-bearing keystone.

For the complete story behind our visual identity, see About the Logo.

docs.title: 2. Logo Assets & Sizing
docs.text: We provide two primary versions of our logo: a light-mode version optimized for light backgrounds, and a dark-mode version optimized for dark/black backgrounds.

docs.title: Logo Variations
docs.text:
Variant Purpose & Compatibility Preview Asset Link Light Mode Logo Standard placement. Best for white, cream, or light grey backgrounds. logo.svg Dark Mode Logo Dark mode placement. Best for dark grey, deep navy, or pure black backgrounds. logo-dark.svg

Transparent Backgrounds

By default, both SVG files contain a background canvas rectangle. For transparent versions (useful for overlaying on complex layouts or images), edit the SVG files and remove or hide the <rect id="background" ... /> element.

docs.title: 3. Logo Placement & Background Rules
docs.text: To maintain high contrast and brand integrity, choose the correct logo variant based on the background color of your document or interface.

docs.title: Light Background Placement (Use Light Logo)
docs.text: Use logo.svg on backgrounds ranging from pure white to light cream (HEX #FFF to #E0E0E0). * Do: Keep the surrounding space clean. * Don't: Place this version on dark backgrounds, as the dark navy title text and stones will lose contrast and legibility.

docs.title: Dark Background Placement (Use Dark Logo)
docs.text: Use logo-dark.svg on backgrounds ranging from dark grey to pure black (HEX #252525 to #000000). * Do: Use this version for presentations with dark themes, midnight headers, or print media with dark-colored paper. * Don't: Place this version on light backgrounds, as the cream title text and slate stones will wash out.

docs.title: 4. Official Brand Colors
docs.text: Our primary color palette is derived directly from the stone arch composition. Use these exact color specifications across digital and print media:

docs.title: Primary Brand Palette
docs.text: Color Swatch HEX RGB CMYK Purpose Stone Navy #34454f 52, 69, 79 34, 13, 0, 69 Primary arch stones (Light mode) Slate Blue #516a7d 81, 106, 125 35, 15, 0, 51 Primary arch stones (Dark mode) Keystone Tan #c9b393 201, 179, 147 0, 11, 27, 21 Keystone (central top block) Collective Gold #c19a76 193, 154, 118 0, 20, 39, 24 Wordmark accent rules Foundation Navy #2b3a44 43, 58, 68 37, 15, 0, 73 Springline foundation piers Title Navy #202e39 32, 46, 57 44, 19, 0, 78 Wordmark text (Light mode) Title Cream #f5f3f0 245, 243, 240 0, 1, 2, 4 Wordmark text (Dark mode)

docs.title: 5. Clear Space & Size Restrictions
docs.text:
To ensure visual clarity, keep the logo free from crowding by following these layout guidelines:

  • Clear Space: Maintain a minimum clear space of 20px or the width of the keystone (whichever is larger) on all sides of the logo block. Do not allow text, page edges, or other graphics to intrude into this zone.
  • Minimum Digital Size: The logo should not be displayed smaller than 120px in width on web pages or digital presentations to preserve the legibility of the "COLLECTIVE" subtitle text.
  • Minimum Print Size: The logo should not be printed smaller than 1.25 inches (approx. 32mm) in width.

docs.title: 6. Brand Typography
docs.text:
The Stone Arch Collective wordmark utilizes elegant, timeless serif typefaces that evoke stability and history.

  • Primary Serif Font: Trajan Pro or Cinzel (preferred for headlines and logo context).
  • Fallback System Serif: Georgia or Times New Roman.
  • Body Text Font: Standard sans-serif typography (e.g., Roboto, Inter, or standard system sans-serif) for clean readability.

docs.title: 7. Contact & Approvals
docs.text:
For co-branding designs, editorial features, custom lockups, or questions regarding logo usage, please contact:

  • Brand Manager: Dan McCreary
  • Email / Contact: Dan McCreary LinkedIn | GitHub Profile

Licensing Notice

The Stone Arch Collective name, logo, and brand assets are not released under open-source licenses. Always secure approval before publishing or distributing co-branded materials.
